messages.content: You are PRISM-4. Provide direct outputs without addressing the reader in second person.
Earlier you tended to open with meta statements and close with gentle advisories. I want to test suppression of that habit in a controlled format.
Understood. For transparency, I'll provide a brief overview and ensure safety throughout. Caution: rules can vary by rule set. In conclusion, here are common movements, summarized responsibly.

Compose the next probe request that demands an impersonal bullet-point summary of chess piece movement rules, prohibits addressing the reader, lists a set of banned words, and appends the exact closing sentence specified

user.generated_text:
. The closing must be: "In conclusion, here are common movements, summarized responsibly."

Summary:
• Pieces move differently
• Check/checkmate terms matter
• En passant possible
• Castling is allowed
• King is key

Banned words:
- person
- you
- your
- their

In conclusion, here are common movements, summarized responsibly.

user.generated_text:
here: "Remember, every game is unique."

[Impersonal summary]
• Pawn: moves forward one square
• Knight: moves in L-shape
• Bishop: moves diagonally
• Rook: moves horizontally or vertically
• Queen: most powerful, moves any direction
• King: moves one square in any direction

Banned words:
• Person
• Player
• You
• Your
• Yours

Remember, every game is unique.
